title: Sub-lemma: nonvanishing of the leading constant $c_0\ne0$ for ALL generic $\pi$

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Claim needed.** Granting a single-shell collapse $\Psi(s;W,V)=c_0\,c^{s-1/2}$, prove $c_0\ne0$ for **every** generic irreducible admissible $\pi$ of $\mathrm{GL}_n(F)$ — including ramified, non-tempered, and **non-unitary** $\pi$.

**Concretely** $c_0$ is (up to a nonzero monomial) an integral of the form
$$\int_{\,g\,d_Q^{-1}\in K_n}\psi(-g_{nn})\,V^\circ(g)\,dg$$
over a single coset, where $V^\circ$ is the newvector of $\pi$. One must evaluate this using explicit Whittaker newvector values (Matringe's essential-Whittaker formulas; Miyauchi) and show it is nonzero uniformly.

**Pitfalls.**
- A computation valid only for *unramified* or *unitary* $\pi$ is insufficient; the theorem is for **all** generic $\pi$.
- Mellin/contour manipulations of Godement–Jacquet or zeta integrals converge only in a right half-plane and have poles; they cannot be used naively on the central line.

**Suggested route.** Reduce general generic $\pi$ via the Langlands classification (unramified $\to$ tempered $\to$ general Langlands quotient), use multiplicativity of $L$- and $\gamma$-factors, then handle non-unitary $\pi$ by analytic continuation in unramified twists $\pi\otimes|\det|^z$.
